title: Hopper Clock
description: Two hoppers face each other and pass items back and forth. A comparator reads one hopper's fill level and outputs a signal each time items arrive. Period is proportional to item count — more items means a slower clock. Far more stable than torch clocks and survives chunk reload without drifting.

steps.title: Stone floor
steps.explanation: Four stone blocks: two under the hoppers, one under the comparator, one under the output lamp. Everything runs at y=1 on this base.

steps.title: Two facing hoppers
steps.explanation: Hopper A at (0,1,0) facing east, Hopper B at (1,1,0) facing west. They output items into each other, creating a loop. Place 1–5 items inside Hopper A to seed the clock — more items make it slower. The clock will not run until seeded.

steps.title: Comparator and output lamp
steps.explanation: Place a comparator at (0,1,1) facing south — its back reads Hopper A to the north, its front outputs south. The lamp at (0,1,2) lights when Hopper A has items and dims when they transfer away, giving you a toggling clock signal. Tap the comparator's south output for any redstone circuit.
